design_philosophy.core_loop: Twin-stick multidirectional shooting through scrolling stages, eliminating waves of enemies and obstacles while collecting power-ups and bombs to progress toward stage bosses
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing dual-stick coordination (movement vs. firing direction) while navigating dense enemy fire and environmental hazards across large scrolling levels
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Embodying elite military operatives (Captain Carnage and Major Mayhem, Rambo-inspired) in a last-ditch military operation to overthrow a dictator and rescue POWs
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Evolves the single-screen twin-stick formula (Smash TV) into scrolling stages with bombs as droppable, delayed-detonation weapons; introduces large enemy vehicles and diverse gameplay scenarios; designed for arcade quarter-feeding through lives/continues system with password warping for staging

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives pool; losing all lives triggers game over unless player inserts credits to continue
difficulty.continues_system: Arcade continue system requiring additional credits to resume from death point
difficulty.checkpointing: Password system allows players to warp to any stage location, providing player agency in difficulty progression
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High: password system enables stage selection and progression pacing control, mitigating some difficulty spikes

progression.upgrade_system: Collectible power-ups and bombs scattered throughout stages; bombs are tactical resources that can be placed and detonated with delay
progression.unlock_mechanism: Three linear stages with mandatory boss fights at stage end; bonus levels (Pleasure Dome) unlocked upon stage completion; subsequent stages accessible only after defeating previous boss
replayability_hooks: Treasure collection in Pleasure Dome bonus stage tied to ending variation (intended good/bad ending system, though buggy in original)
Two-player cooperative play encouraging different strategies and teamwork
Password system enables speedrunning specific stages or challenge modes

world_building_techniques: Setting in fictional country of Kookistan during 1999 with dictator General Akhboob (Saddam Hussein reference)
Military aesthetic and character design (Rambo-inspired operatives from Doomsday Squad)
Real-world geopolitical influence (1991 Persian Gulf War, Abu Ghraib infant formula plant reference)
POW rescue objective provides narrative framing for stage progression
Enemy mutant army establishes antagonistic world

boss_design.philosophy: Mandatory progression gates; each of three stages culminates in a boss fight before advancing; boss Orcus appears as first major encounter

faction_indicators: Doomsday Squad (player faction: elite military operatives)
POW resistance (implied allied faction to rescue)
danger_signals: Dictatorial regime conquest threat to world
Mutant army occupation and military presence
POW detention indicating brutal authoritarian control
environmental_storytelling: Akhboob's 'Baby Milk Factory' base—militarized civilian infrastructure as evidence of regime callousness
Scrolling stage geography reveals military occupation and fortification
Enemy vehicle prevalence suggests heavy mechanized occupation
